prompt:
Replace the existing CSS file at style.css with the new beautified UI version located at /home/patrick/Downloads/flux-style.css.

Steps:
1. Read /home/patrick/Downloads/flux-style.css — it's a 793-line beautified UI replacement for style.css (the existing style.css is 1026 lines). The new file's header comment says: 'Flux — Beautified UI (replaces style.css). Single coherent design with dark/light only. Existing data-theme values still resolve (no breakage).'
2. Copy its contents over the repo's style.css (just 'cp /home/patrick/Downloads/flux-style.css style.css' from the repo root, or read+write via your tools).
3. DO NOT modify any other files unless something breaks. The existing app.js, index.html, manifest.json, etc. should keep working because the new CSS is meant to be a drop-in replacement that preserves the data-theme/data-mode attribute API.
4. Run the test suite: 'nix develop -c npm test' or whatever the project's test command is — check README.md / package.json / flake.nix for the right command. The repo has 106 e2e Playwright tests per recent PR descriptions. All must pass.
5. If tests fail because of CSS-dependent selectors (e.g. tests grep for specific class names that no longer exist), investigate and report — don't blindly modify tests. If a test failure is legitimately caused by the new CSS removing/renaming something the app depends on, that's a bug in the new CSS and you should report rather than band-aid.

Acceptance criteria:
- style.css replaced with the contents of /home/patrick/Downloads/flux-style.css
- All existing tests still pass
- No other files modified
- PR title: 'style: replace style.css with beautified UI'
- PR body: brief summary plus a note that this enables a fly.io preview deployment via the Deploy Flux Preview workflow chain (flux → infra → armstrong reusable workflow), so the reviewer can visually inspect at https://flux-preview-<PR>.fly.dev/ once CI passes.
